children.label: A
children.text: that a landowner voluntarily agrees to sell to a State; and

children.label: I
children.text: is ineligible for enrollment as a wetland reserve easement established under the agricultural conservation easement program under subtitle H of the Food Security Act of 1985;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: aa
children.text: an average depth of at least 6.5 feet; or

children.label: bb
children.text: a level below which the State determines the management of the water level is beyond the control of the State or landowner; or

children.label: III
children.text: is inaccessible for agricultural use due to the flooding of adjoining property (such as islands of agricultural land created by flooding);

children.label: ii
children.text: is located within a watershed with water rights available for lease or purchase; and

children.label: I
children.text: to produce crops or hay; or

children.label: II
children.text: as livestock pasture or grazing.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 2 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the voluntary land purchase program established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: considered flooded because there is no natural outlet for water accumulating in the lake or the associated riparian area such that the watershed and surrounding land is consistently flooded; or

children.label: B
children.text: considered terminal because it has no natural outlet and is at risk due to a history of consistent Federal assistance to address critical resource conditions, including insufficient water available to meet the needs of the lake, general uses, and water rights.
provide grants under subsection (c) for the purchase of eligible land impacted by a terminal lake described in subsection (a)(3)(A); and
provide funds to the Secretary of the Interior pursuant to subsection (e)(2) with assistance in accordance with subsection (d) for terminal lakes described in subsection (a)(3)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Using funds provided under subsection (e)(1), the Secretary shall make available land purchase grants to States for the purchase of eligible land in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of the total purchase price per acre of the eligible land; or

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of eligible land that was used to produce crops or hay, $400 per acre; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of eligible land that was pasture or grazing land, $200 per acre.

children.heading: Determination of purchase price
children.label: B Determination of purchase price
children.text: A State purchasing eligible land with a land purchase grant shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that the purchase price of such land reflects the value, if any, of other encumbrances on the eligible land to be purchased, including easements and mineral rights.

children.heading: Cost-share required
children.label: C Cost-share required
children.text: To be eligible to receive a land purchase grant, a State shall provide matching non-Federal funds in an amount equal to 50 percent of the amount described in subparagraph (A), including additional non-Federal funds.

children.label: I
children.text: conveyed in fee simple to the State; and

children.label: II
children.text: free from mortgages or other liens at the time title is transferred;

children.label: ii
children.text: to maintain ownership of the eligible land in perpetuity;

children.label: iii
children.text: to pay (from funds other than grant dollars awarded) any costs associated with the purchase of eligible land under this section, including surveys and legal fees; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to keep eligible land in a conserving use, as defined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: benefits under title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: ii
children.text: benefits under the Federal Crop Insurance Act ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 1501

children.label: iii
children.text: covered benefits described in section 1001D(b) of the Food Security Act of 1985 ().7 U.S.C. 1308–3a

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: F Prohibition
children.text: Any Federal rights or benefits associated with eligible land prior to purchase by a State may not be transferred to any other land or person in anticipation of or as a result of such purchase.

children.label: A
children.text: to lease water;

children.label: B
children.text: to purchase land, water appurtenant to the land, and related interests; and

children.label: C
children.text: to carry out research, support, and conservation activities for associated fish, wildlife, plant, and habitat resources.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: 2 Exclusions
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may not use this subsection to deliver assistance to the Great Salt Lake in Utah, lakes that are considered dry lakes, or other lakes that do not meet the purposes of this section, as determined by the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, any funds made available before , under a provision of law described in subparagraph (B) shall remain available using the provisions of law (including regulations) in effect on the day before .February 7, 2014February 7, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: section 2507 of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 ( note; ) (as in effect on the day before );43 U.S.C. 2211Public Law 107–171February 7, 2014

children.label: ii
children.text: section 207 of the Energy and Water Development Appropriations Act, 2003 (; );Public Law 108–7117 Stat. 146

children.label: iii
children.text: section 208 of the Energy and Water Development Appropriations Act, 2006 (; , ); andPublic Law 109–103119 Stat. 2268123 Stat. 2856

children.label: iv
children.text: section 208 of the Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2010 (; , , ).Public Law 111–85123 Stat. 2858123 Stat. 2967125 Stat. 867

children.heading: Commodity Credit Corporation
children.label: 1 Commodity Credit Corporation
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall transfer to the “Bureau of Reclamation—Water and Related Resources” account $150,000,000 from the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation to carry out subsection (d), to remain available until expended.February 7, 2014

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Nothing in this section authorizes any additional funds to carry out this section.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: B Availability of funds
children.text: Any funds made available to carry out this section before , may remain available until expended.December 20, 2018

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: f Termination of authority
children.text: The authority provided by this section shall terminate on .October 1, 2025
